title: Establishment Office Order - Departmental Promotion
content:
GOVERNMENT OF INDIA
MINISTRY OF PERSONNEL, PUBLIC GRIEVANCES AND PENSIONS
DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL & TRAINING

No. 12011/04/2026-Estt.(B)
New Delhi, Dated the 14th April, 2026

OFFICE MEMORANDUM

Subject: Regular promotion of Assistant Section Officers (ASOs) of Central Secretariat Service (CSS) to Section Officer Grade for the year 2026.

The undersigned is directed to refer to this Department's Office Memorandum of even number dated 5th March, 2026, on the subject mentioned above, and to state that the President is pleased to approve the regular promotion of 42 Assistant Section Officers to Section Officer Grade in the Pay Matrix Level-8 (Rs. 47,600 - 1,51,100) with immediate effect.

The officers so promoted are directed to report to their new Ministries of postings as listed in Annexure-I within ten working days. Failure to join the promoted post within the stipulated period will attract rules on debarment from future promotions as per regulations.

Signed,
(R. K. Sharma)
Under Secretary to the Government of India

title: Administrative Circular on Office Attendance & Biometrics
content:
OFFICE OF THE CHIEF SECRETARY
GOVERNMENT OF JAMMU & KASHMIR
CIVIL SECRETARIAT, SRINAGAR

Circular No. 12-JK(GAD) of 2026
Dated: 12.05.2026

Subject: Attendance through Aadhaar-Enabled Biometric Attendance System (AEBAS).

Attention is drawn to all administrative secretaries and heads of departments regarding punctuality in civil offices. It has been observed with serious concern that several employees are not registering their attendance on the AEBAS, resulting in laxity in administrative functions. This has severely impacted delivery of public services.

It is hereby ordered that all government servants working in various departments, civil secretariats, and public sector undertakings shall mark their attendance on AEBAS daily without exception. Drawing and Disbursing Officers (DDOs) shall verify the monthly biometric attendance reports before preparing salary bills. Salary for employees failing to mark biometric attendance shall be kept on hold till explanation.

By Order of the Government,
General Administration Department

title: Supreme Court Judgement - Writ Petition No. 402
content:
IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CIVIL APPELLATE JURISDICTION
WRIT PETITION (CIVIL) NO. 402 OF 2025

In the Matter of:
Association for Democratic Reforms & Anr.
...Petitioners

Versus
Union of India & Ors.
...Respondents

JUDGEMENT

1. This Writ Petition filed under Article 32 of the Constitution of India raises a significant constitutional question regarding the right to information of electors vis-a-vis the disclosure of funding of political parties. The petitioner contends that the amendment to Section 29C of the Representation of the People Act, 1951, through the Finance Act, 2017, violates the basic structure of the Constitution.

2. We have heard the learned Senior Counsel for the parties and examined the relevant provisions of the Income Tax Act, 1961, and the Companies Act, 2013. The Constitution does not explicitly list the rights of voters, but Article 19(1)(a) has been interpreted as encompassing the voter's right to secure essential information. An informed citizen is better equipped to exercise franchise in a democratic setup. Hence, the petition succeeds.

title: Ministry of Finance - Dearness Allowance Revision
content:
No. 1/1/2026-E-II (B)
Government of India
Ministry of Finance
Department of Expenditure

North Block, New Delhi
Dated the 22nd March, 2026

OFFICE MEMORANDUM

Subject: Rate of Dearness Allowance (DA) for Central Government Employees - Revised effective from 01.01.2026.

The undersigned is directed to refer to this Department's Office Memorandum No. 1/3/2025-E-II (B) dated 12th September, 2025, and to state that the President is pleased to decide that the Dearness Allowance payable to Central Government employees shall be enhanced from the existing rate of fifty percent (50%) to fifty-four percent (54%) of the Basic Pay with effect from 1st January, 2026.

The term 'Basic Pay' in the revised pay structure means the pay drawn in the prescribed Level in the Pay Matrix but does not include any other type of pay like special pay. The arrears shall be paid in cash.

(Amit Kumar)
Director

title: General Essay on Digital India Initiative
content: The Digital India campaign was launched by the Government of India to ensure that government services are made available to citizens electronically by improved online infrastructure and by increasing Internet connectivity. The program has three core components: the creation of a stable digital infrastructure, delivering services digitally, and universal digital literacy. Since its inception, Digital India has revolutionized transactions across the nation. Unified Payments Interface (UPI) is a prime example of this success, enabling millions of street vendors, shops, and large supermarkets to receive instant bank transfers on mobile devices. This has minimized cash handling, boosted digital transparency, and brought millions of unbanked citizens into the formal financial sector. High-speed broadband connections are currently being extended to over two hundred thousand Gram Panchayats. This massive rural expansion ensures that students, farmers, and small businesses in remote villages can access national markets and e-learning resources directly without middlemen.

title: Statistical Report on Railway Passenger Traffic
content: Passenger traffic in Indian Railways showed significant recovery in the fiscal year 2025-2026. The total number of originating passengers registered was 6,520 million compared to 5,840 million in the previous year, showing a growth of 11.6%. Earnings from passenger reservations reached Rs. 62,450.50 crores, a substantial increase from Rs. 54,320.10 crores in the prior period. Zone-wise distribution showed Northern Railway (NR) leading with 1,210.5 million passengers, while Southern Railway (SR) registered 854.2 million. The average lead distance traveled per passenger was 342.5 kilometers, up by 2.4%. Goods loading reached an all-time high of 1,410.60 million tonnes, yielding a freight revenue of Rs. 1,62,400 crores. These numbers reflect strong infrastructure spending and continuous expansion of dedicated freight corridors.

title: Structured Data Representation and Formatting
Technical Document with Special Symbols
content: The system parameters must satisfy: [Alpha-Value] => {Gamma} * [Delta-2] / (Beta_3) <= 98.75%. In our current trial (#923-B), we observed that under high load (~2500 requests/sec), the performance ratio dropped by delta = -0.15. Check if (x + y)^2 != x^2 + y^2 when x, y in [-10, 10]. The mathematical bounds are bounded by epsilon = 0.005. The formula is: f(x) = (pi * r^2) / (2 * L) + theta_0. Please ensure the keyboard layout supports standard ANSI symbols: `~ ! @ # $ % ^ & * ( ) _ + { } | : " < > ? - = [ ] \ ; ' , . /. The ratio between A and B must strictly stay at 1.618 : 1.
